case.title: PEOPLE OF STATE OF NEW YORK EX REL BLAGDEN v. LYNCH

opinion.h1: PEOPLE OF STATE OF NEW YORK EX REL BLAGDEN v. LYNCH, 292 U.S. 602 (1934)
opinion.h2: The PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK, ex rel. Dexter BLAGDEN, appellant, v. Thomas M. LYNCH, John J. Merrill, and John P. Hennessey, as Tex Commissioners of the State of New York.* No. 905.
Supreme Court of the United States
opinion.p: Mr. Sidney W. Davidson, of New York City, for appellant.
For decision below, see 263 N.Y. 568, 189 N.E. 701.

The appeal is dismissed for the want of jurisdiction. Section 237(a), Judicial Code, as amended by the Act of February 13, 1925 (43 Stat. 936, 937, 28 USCA 344(a). Treating the papers whereon the appeal was allowed as a petition for writ of certiorari, as required by section 237(c), Judicial Code, as amended (43 Stat. 939, 938, 28 USCA 344(c), certiorari is denied.
* Rehearing denied 292 U.S. 608, 54 S. Ct. 772, 78 L. Ed. --.[ People of State of New York ex rel Blagden v. Lynch 292 U.S. 602 (1934) ]
